chunks.text: Technical Reports - Vladimir Stojanovic. Towards a Wireless Fluorescence Microscope on A Chip (EECS-2025-31) Rozhan Rabbani. Cryogenic Optical Link: Device, Circuit, and System (EECS-2025-28) Bozhi Yin. A Wireless Image Sensor for Real-Time, In Vivo Fluorescence Microscopy in Cancer Therapy (EECS-2024-29) Rozhan Rabbani. Silicon Microring Resonator Design for Monolithic Electronic–Photonic Biosensing System () Hyeong Seok Oh and Vladimir Stojanovic. Fully Integrated Electronic-Biophotonic System-on-Chip for Real-Time Label-Free Molecular Sensing (EECS-2023-241) Christos Adamopoulos.
chunks.question: What recent technical reports are listed for Vladimir Stojanovic's group?
chunks.answer: Wireless fluorescence microscopes and integrated biosensing systems.

chunks.text: Realization of a Fully Integrated Electronic-Photonic Sensor-Receiver Array for Endoscopic Ultrasound (EECS-2023-35) Panagiotis Zarkos. Berkeley Open MOS dataBase (BOMB): A Dataset for Silicon Technology Representation Learning (EECS-2021-192) Rohan Lageweg. Ultrasound Detection with Silicon Microring Resonators (EECS-2021-123) Sarika Madhvapathy.
chunks.question: What applications do the 2021-2023 technical reports focus on?
chunks.answer: Ultrasound, LIDAR, energy-efficient links, and datasets.

chunks.text: Enhancing Performance of Analog Mixed Signal Circuits using Integrated Silicon-Photonics (EECS-2020-195) Nandish Mehta. A Fully Integrated Electronic-Photonic Platform for Label-Free Biosensing (EECS-2020-15) Christos Adamopoulos. Closing the Analog Design Loop with the Berkeley Analog Generator (EECS-2019-23) Nicholas Werblun.
chunks.question: What are key themes in the 2019-2020 technical reports?
chunks.answer: Analog design, silicon photonics, and biosensing platforms.

chunks.text: Photonic Links -- From Theory to Automated Design (EECS-2019-7) Vladimir Stojanovic. chunks.question: What photonic technologies are covered in 2017-2019 reports?
chunks.answer: Optical transceivers, chip-to-chip links, and high-speed data.

chunks.answer: Patents, memory interfaces, and DRAM with photonics.
